claim: The private archive contains 238 raw option files across seven named assets.
recomputation: Count root data files matching the exact regular expression ^[A-Z]+[0-9]{8}\\.csv$.
caveat: Three root CSV work files do not match the definition; licensed data are not published.

claim: The private archive contains 559 auxiliary CSV files.
recomputation: Recursively count auxiliary CSV files after excluding raw option files.
caveat: Coverage and provenance vary by auxiliary table.

claim: Available raw coverage spans AAPL INTC NDX NVDA RUT SPX and SPY with irregular dates from 2024-07-01 through 2025-08-29.
recomputation: Group raw filenames by ticker and compute minimum and maximum dates.
caveat: This is not a random or continuous historical sample.

claim: European parity regression identifies discount and forward by D=-beta and F=alpha/D from C-P=alpha+beta K.
recomputation: Compare regression signs and discount conventions against synthetic parity cases.
caveat: Requires common-strike call-put support and European exercise semantics.

claim: Canonical Black implied volatility uses a bracketed Brent solve with initial bounds 1e-6 and 3 controlled expansion to 5 and 8 tolerance 1e-8 and 80 iterations.
recomputation: Run synthetic Black round trips across option type maturity moneyness and volatility including invalid-bound cases.
caveat: American canonical calibration still uses this Black normalization.

claim: Raw SVI models total variance as a+b[rho(k-m)+sqrt((k-m)^2+sigma_svi^2)].
recomputation: Evaluate formula and gradients against finite differences and known parameter sets.
caveat: Parameter and proxy constraints do not prove continuous global no-arbitrage.

claim: The promoted Jacobian-consistent weight is proportional to [vega/(2 sigma T half_spread)]^2.
recomputation: Numerically perturb quote price and compare induced total-variance variance with the analytic Jacobian approximation.
caveat: Requires floors and guards near zero vega spread volatility or maturity.

claim: Early-exercise premium is computed as a matched-input American price minus European price.
recomputation: Reprice matched synthetic contracts with American and European exercise flags and verify EEP identity.
caveat: The decomposition is numerical and model-conditional.

claim: Pooled is reporting-only and the European calibration families are monthly_am_standard monthly_pm_standard weekly_pm_nonstandard eom_pm and quarter_end_pm.
recomputation: Generate dates roots settlement flags and expiry indicators around monthly month-end and quarter-end boundaries.
caveat: American routing has separately preserved semantics.

claim: The guarded weekly refinement changed 7 of 24 frozen asset-date slices.
recomputation: Join repaired-grid full-supported RMSE by date ticker and basket; count unique date-ticker keys with absolute difference above 1e-9.
caveat: Selective activation is not evidence of a global model replacement.

claim: Frozen repaired weekly mean RMSE fell from 7.4178796661 to 3.8994934737 on the liquidity basket.
recomputation: Filter repaired-grid weekly_pm_nonstandard and liquidity_kbins then average RMSE over 24 asset-date rows in each panel.
caveat: Raw price units differ materially across assets.

claim: Frozen repaired weekly mean RMSE fell from 8.6654054814 to 3.9681363322 on the representative basket.
recomputation: Filter repaired-grid weekly_pm_nonstandard and representative_stratified then average RMSE over 24 asset-date rows in each panel.
caveat: Raw price units differ materially across assets.

claim: Full-supported-chain repaired weekly mean RMSE fell from 6.3035949494 to 3.1699631679 with no losses among changed rows.
scope: SPX NDX RUT weekly_pm_nonstandard full-supported chain
recomputation: Filter repaired-grid and weekly_pm_nonstandard; average full_supported_rmse_python over 48 basket rows; join controls and count positive deltas above 1e-9 among changed rows.
caveat: Basket rows duplicate the same full chain across two basket modes; report denominator explicitly.

claim: Repair-aware eSSVI reruns did not support a global replacement but did produce a local monthly_pm_standard candidate.
recomputation: Recompute paired and tail gates by atomic family with matched repair states.
caveat: A candidate is not a promoted canonical policy.

claim: Direct-price fitting improved some quote repricing metrics but failed diagnostic or tail requirements for global promotion.
recomputation: Recompute common-support price errors and feasibility gates for all direct-price variants.
caveat: Do not summarize optimization fit alone as pricing superiority.

claim: The corrected no-repair rerun is admissible and the first no-repair run is invalid due to path contamination.
recomputation: Compare manifest paths output hashes and repair flags; exclude the first run from numerical aggregation.
caveat: Publication should explain invalidation without using contaminated values.

claim: The carry audit covers 357 expiries and reports a mean cross-validation overfit ratio of 1.0429822198.
recomputation: Read expiry count and mean_cv_overfit_ratio from the aggregate row and recompute from expiry detail.
caveat: CV denotes deterministic two-fold interleaved-strike cross-validation; the ratio alone is not a causal test.

claim: Mean absolute log-forward shift in the carry audit is 0.0337422463 basis points.
recomputation: Recompute the mean absolute log-forward difference from expiry detail using documented basis-point conversion.
caveat: Applies only to audited expiries and carry perturbation.

claim: The carry audit records zero selection flips and zero keep/drop flips under its aggregate definitions.
recomputation: Verify aggregate selection and keep/drop columns against detail rows.
caveat: Column semantics must be retained in the public field dictionary.

claim: The basket audit candidate contains 96 contexts and an average export-to-full-supported row-count ratio of 0.0598296205.
recomputation: Recompute count and mean export_vs_full_count_ratio from basket_selection_detail.csv.
caveat: Export and full-chain scores answer different sampling questions.

claim: The principal adverse weekly target was not explained primarily by endpoint or extrapolation contracts.
scope: NDX focused and November weekly panels
recomputation: Recompute common-support share and boundary squared-error fractions from quote-level audit outputs.
caveat: Boundary behavior can remain important in other regimes.

claim: American-aware normalization challengers did not justify broad replacement of baseline normalization across AAPL SPY and INTC panels.
recomputation: Recompute matched-support feasibility repricing and tail deltas for each normalization method and asset.
caveat: Evidence is limited to saved panels and canonical calibration still uses Black IV.

claim: CRR with 400 steps differed by at most about 0.00138 from the 3200-step CRR reference on the two SPY convergence contracts.
recomputation: Filter engine_family=crr and resolution=400 then take max abs_to_family_reference.
caveat: This is panel evidence not a universal error bound.

claim: The promoted 1600 by 1600 scale-4 CN-PSOR setup differs by about 0.00524 and 0.00060 from the CRR reference prices on the two SPY convergence contracts.
recomputation: Filter case=fd_grid_1600_scale_4p0 and compute absolute modelPrice minus crr_reference_price by contract.
caveat: CRR is a high-step numerical reference here not ground truth.

claim: The closing bundle completed 48 of 48 asset-date contexts and produced 2400 valid prices out of 2400 rows.
recomputation: Verify expected ticker-date Cartesian count and count valid rows in pricing_book.csv.
caveat: Completion does not imply strict deployment pass.

claim: Aggregate raw-dollar pricing RMSE is 3.5121966299 MAE is 1.4620095120 and maximum absolute error is 27.7367511151.
recomputation: Recompute from model minus market price over all 2400 valid rows.
caveat: Dollar aggregation is asset-scale dependent.

claim: Quote-quality pass warning and failure rates are 0.7391666667 0.2579166667 and 0.0029166667.
recomputation: Count quote_quality status over 2400 rows and divide by denominator.
caveat: Threshold equality semantics matter for the seven failures.

claim: The closing bundle has no engine-disagreement failures and no below-intrinsic rows.
recomputation: Count material engine-disagreement and below-intrinsic flags.
caveat: Depends on current absolute and relative materiality rules.

claim: The 24 American contexts have complete pair coverage mean early-exercise premium 0.0042642779 and no negative-premium classification.
scope: INTC NVDA SPY over eight dates
recomputation: Filter American contexts; verify matched pairs and aggregate premium and negative classifications.
caveat: Premium is model-conditional and averaged across heterogeneous contracts.

claim: The signal layer emits 414 rows and 61 alerts consisting of 38 review 23 watch and zero high alerts.
recomputation: Count signal rows and alert priorities.
caveat: Signals are descriptive and not validated alpha.

claim: The strict deployment validator fails with one failure and two warnings because seven quotes equal the 40 percent relative-spread limit.
recomputation: Read validation status then filter quote-quality failures and verify relative_spread equals threshold under configured comparison semantics.
caveat: This is a liquidity-policy failure and must not be relabeled as an engine failure.

claim: An independent Phase 1 rerun passed all 195 Python tests.
recomputation: Run python -m unittest discover -s tests -p test_*.py from the frozen revision.
caveat: Passing tests do not substitute for independent model validation.

claim: An independent clean C++ configure and release build completed and CTest passed one of one tests.
source_artifact: CMake build and CTest output on 2026-07-12
recomputation: Configure in a fresh out-of-tree directory build Release and run CTest with output on failure.
caveat: Coverage depth is limited despite pass status.

claim: The smoothed local-window repo branch retained high common support but worsened the aggregate repaired-grid comparison despite local context wins.
scope: five atomic European families on the frozen August panel
recomputation: Inner-join contracts privately, export only context sufficient statistics, then count contexts and wins.
caveat: Raw-price scale is asset dependent and context wins do not imply aggregate promotion.

claim: The restructured public release passed 48 Python tests and six CTest suites in its final reseal.
recomputation: Read final clean-environment test counts and rerun before release.
caveat: Passing software tests is not independent economic model validation.
